author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "For when you in your guilt perform what is false, I do not celebrate baptism twice, which you have never celebrated once."

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: In the first place, you do not convict us of guilt.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: And if a guilty man baptizes with a false baptism, then none of those have true baptism who are baptized by men in your party, that are, I do not say openly, but even secretly guilty.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: For if he who gives baptism gives something that is God’s, if he is already guilty in the sight of God, how can he be giving something that is God’s if a guilty man cannot give true baptism?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But in reality you wait till he is guilty in your sight as well, as though what he proposes to confer were something that belonged to you.
